content: Direct comparisons between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning are only meaningful if we account for how a generic agent’s working memory capacity shapes the encoding of prediction errors that power both intrinsic reward signals. Agents with larger state representation buffers will show reversed performance gaps between ICM and RND compared to agents with tight memory constraints.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
